```{r}
# [1] "mt-Nd3~expr, pvalue=0.00256466229904208, rho=0.836363636363636"
# [1] "mt-Nd3~Abeta, pvalue=0.225014088667236, rho=0.4"

# [1] "mt-Nd4~log2fc, pvalue=0.0254755837807706, rho=0.681818181818182"
# [1] "mt-Nd4~expr, pvalue=0.000225324584283125, rho=0.9"
# [1] "mt-Nd4~Abeta, pvalue=0.172786506584626, rho=0.445454545454545"

# [1] "mt-Co3~log2fc, pvalue=0.000884153869770606, rho=0.851393052514601"
# [1] "mt-Co3~expr, pvalue=0.000948977059617062, rho=0.872727272727273"
# [1] "mt-Co3~Abeta, pvalue=0.248380568071137, rho=0.381818181818182"

# [1] "mt-Atp6~log2fc, pvalue=0.0114567316577406, rho=0.725777684110807"
# [1] "mt-Atp6~expr, pvalue=5.55408265736478e-05, rho=0.909090909090909"
# [1] "mt-Atp6~Abeta, pvalue=0.106918151178142, rho=0.518181818181818"

# [1] "Ndufa3~log2fc, pvalue=0.380889759521277, rho=0.293590337367029"
# [1] "Ndufa3~expr, pvalue=0.0403864039827663, rho=0.636363636363636"
# [1] "Ndufa3~Abeta, pvalue=0.653538937959263, rho=0.154545454545455"

# [1] "Ndufa5~log2fc, pvalue=0.091991920043484, rho=0.532132486477741"
# [1] "Ndufa5~expr, pvalue=0.468183503088795, rho=0.245454545454545"
# [1] "Ndufa5~Abeta, pvalue=0.503066960517545, rho=-0.227272727272727"

# [1] "Cox8a~log2fc, pvalue=0.179847045399238, rho=0.436199805445017"
# [1] "Cox8a~expr, pvalue=0.182531860964529, rho=0.436363636363636"
# [1] "Cox8a~Abeta, pvalue=0.576257826919622, rho=0.190909090909091"

# [1] "Cox7c~log2fc, pvalue=0.0444240278542043, rho=0.614119578862991"
# [1] "Cox7c~expr, pvalue=0.0280703042358757, rho=0.672727272727273"
# [1] "Cox7c~Abeta, pvalue=0.371067789395025, rho=0.3"


# Candidate genes (where the p-value of the LFC correlation coefficient is less than that of the expression level correlation coefficient): log2fc(mt-Atp8) ~ log2fc(mt-Nd3) is more significant than expr(mt-Atp8) ~ expr(mt-Nd3), Ndufa5

# No genes showed a significant correlation between log2fc(mt-Atp8) and Abeta_index, even before multiplex correction.

# Conclusion: Changes in oxidative phosphorylation are brain region-specific, but not associated with Aβ.

# Drawing the image:

# log2fc(mt-Nd3) ~ log2fc(mt-Atp8)

# expr(mt-Atp8) ~ expr(mt-Nd3)

# log2fc(mt-Atp8) ~ expr(mt-Nd3)

# Enlarging the dots and using their colors to represent brain regions might yield better results.

gene1 <- "mt-Atp8"
gene2 <- "mt-Nd3"
gene2 <- "Ndufa5"
gene2 <- "Ndufa3"
gene2 <- "Cox7c"

meta.st[[gene1]] <- expr.st[gene1, ]
meta.st[[gene2]] <- expr.st[gene2, ]
meta.st.long2 <- melt(meta.st, id.vars=c("treatment", "cell_types"), 
                     measure.vars=c("Abeta_index", gene1, gene2), 
                     variable.name="gene", value.name="expr")
meta.st.agg2 <- aggregate(expr~treatment + cell_types + gene, FUN=mean,
                          data=meta.st.long2)
df1 <- meta.st.agg2[meta.st.agg2$gene==gene1 & meta.st.agg2$treatment=="PLX5622", ]
df2 <- meta.st.agg2[meta.st.agg2$gene==gene2 & meta.st.agg2$treatment=="PLX5622", ]

outdir <- "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/OXPHOS"
dir.create(outdir)


lfc1 <- get_lfc(gene1)
lfc2 <- get_lfc(gene2)
df.expr <- data.frame(brain_region=df1$cell_types, expr1=df1$expr, expr2=df2$expr)
row.names(df.expr) <- df.expr$brain_region
df.expr <- df.expr[brain_regions, ]
df.expr$lfc1 <- lfc1
df.expr$lfc2 <- lfc2

o1 <- cor.test(df.expr$lfc1, df.expr$lfc2, method="spearman") # p=0, rho=0.9181818
o2 <- cor.test(df.expr$expr1, df.expr$lfc2, method="spearman") # p=0.002078, rho=0.8454545
o3 <- cor.test(df.expr$lfc1, df.expr$expr2, method="spearman") # p=0.0168, rho=0.7181818

mycolorcode = c("Isocortex"="#666666",
                "Fiber tracts"="#E7298A",
                "Thalamus"="#FF7F00",
                "Olfactory area"="#E41A1C",
                "Hypothalamus"="#377EB8",
                "Hippocampus"="#4DAF4A",
                "Cortical subplate"="#A65628",
                "Meninges"="#80B1D3",
                "Striatum"="#B3DE69",
                "Lateral Ventricle"="#D9D9D9",
                "Third Ventricle"="#BC80BD",
                "C12"="#FFED6F")

p1 <- ggplot(df.expr, aes(lfc1, lfc2)) + 
  geom_point(aes(color=brain_region), size=2) + geom_smooth(method="lm") + 
  labs(x=paste0("log2fc(", gene1, ")"), y=paste0("log2fc(", gene2, ")")) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=mycolorcode) + 
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank(), 
        legend.position="none")

# p2 <- ggplot(df.expr, aes(expr1, expr2)) + 
#   geom_point(size=0.2) + geom_smooth(method="lm") + 
#   labs(x=paste0("expr(", gene1, ")"), y=paste0("expr(", gene2, ")")) + 
#   theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank())

p3 <- ggplot(df.expr, aes(expr1, lfc2)) + 
  geom_point(aes(color=brain_region), size=2) + geom_smooth(method="lm") + 
  labs(x=paste0("expr(", gene1, ")"), y=paste0("log2fc(", gene2, ")")) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=mycolorcode) + 
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank())

library(patchwork)
p <- p1 | p3

# ggsave(paste0(outdir, "/", gene1, ".", gene2, ".lfc.png"), p1, width=2.5, height=2.5)
# ggsave(paste0(outdir, "/", gene1, ".", gene2, ".expr.png"), p2, width=2.5, height=2.5)
# ggsave(paste0(outdir, "/", gene1, ".", gene2, ".expr_lfc.png"), p3, width=2.5, height=2.5)

ggsave(paste0(outdir, "/", gene1, ".", gene2, ".png"), p, width=4*2, height=3.5)
```

## 6 Organize the GFAP and P2RY12 index tables

Two batches, each with 3 individuals, totaling 6 tables. These need to be merged together.

```{r}
outdir <- "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence"
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta.csv", row.names=1)

#############################################################################

treatments <- c("FADCtrl", "WTCtrl", "PLX5622")

for (i.gene in c("P2ry12", "Gfap")){
  measure.list <- list()
  n <- 1
  for (treatment in treatments){
    for (batch in c("1", "2")){
      sample <- paste0(treatment, "_", batch)
      cat("doing sample:", sample, ";", "gene:", i.gene, "\n")
      if (batch == "1"){
        save.dir <- file.path(outdir, "measurement")
        if (i.gene == "P2ry12") {
          channel <- "ch01"
        } else if (i.gene == "Gfap"){
          channel <- "ch00"
        }
        infile <- file.path(save.dir, 
                            treatment, 
                            paste0("Results.", channel, ".reshaped.csv"))
      } else if (batch == "2") {
        save.dir <- file.path(outdir, "batch2")
        if (i.gene == "P2ry12") {
          channel <- "ch03"
        } else if (i.gene == "Gfap"){
          channel <- "ch04"
        }
        infile <- file.path(save.dir, 
                            treatment, 
                            paste0("Results.", channel, ".reshaped.csv"))
      }
      measure <- read.csv(infile, row.names=1)
      measure$barcode <- paste0(sample, "-", measure$barcode)
      
      row.names(measure) <- measure$barcode
      measure.list[[n]] <- measure
      n <- n + 1
    }
  }
  measure.df <- do.call(rbind, measure.list)
  meta[[i.gene]] <- measure.df[meta$cell_ID, "Mean"]
}

write.csv(meta, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv")
```

## Cell Types Related to GFAP

1. Read the GFAP index table.

2. Read the per-spot cell proportion table.

3. Correlation analysis.


```{r}

meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)

df.deconvo <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/cell2location/level2/spot.anno.prop.csv", row.names=1)
meta <- merge(meta, df.deconvo, by.x="cell_ID", by.y="cell.id")

proteins <- c("Gfap", "P2ry12", "Abeta_index")
cells <- colnames(meta)[18:54]

protein.vec <- c()
cell.vec <- c()
p.vec <- c()
rho.vec <- c()
n <- 1
for (protein in proteins) {
  for (cell in cells) {
    r <- cor.test(meta[[protein]], meta[[cell]], method="spearman")
    protein.vec[n] <- protein
    cell.vec[n] <- cell
    p.vec[n] <- r$p.value
    rho.vec[n] <- r$estimate
    n <- n + 1
  }
}

out.df <- data.frame(protein=protein.vec, cell_type=cell.vec, pvalue=p.vec, rho=rho.vec) 
out.df <- out.df[order(out.df$pvalue), ]
write.csv(out.df, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/GFAP_P2RY12_Abeta_index__cor__cell_types.csv")

head(out.df, 10)
#         protein cell_type        pvalue       rho
# 29         Gfap Micro_0_1  0.000000e+00 0.3676464
# 65       P2ry12 Micro_0_0  0.000000e+00 0.3390935
# 23         Gfap     Glu_5 1.410049e-291 0.2652368
# 103 Abeta_index Micro_0_1 6.194182e-278 0.2591298
# 67       P2ry12 Micro_0_2 4.995678e-263 0.2522634
# 30         Gfap Micro_0_2 2.821899e-250 0.2462165
# 19         Gfap    Glu_12 7.900699e-249 0.2455197
# 99  Abeta_index     Glu_7 1.095206e-235 0.2390866
# 24         Gfap     Glu_6 6.136448e-191 0.2155707
# 16         Gfap     Glu_1 5.137212e-188 0.2139352

```


## Genes related to GFAP 2

Previously, correlation analysis was performed on each sample separately. Actually, multiple samples can be combined for correlation analysis.

```{r}
library(Giotto)
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)

df.deconvo <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/cell2location/level2/spot.anno.prop.csv", row.names=1)
meta <- merge(meta, df.deconvo, by.x="cell_ID", by.y="cell.id")

meta.gobj <- meta[match(pDataDT(gobj)$cell_ID, meta$cell_ID), ] 
all(meta.gobj$cell_ID == pDataDT(gobj)$cell_ID) # True

# Perform a correlation analysis on each gene and the GFAP index.

expr <- get_expression_values(gobj, output="matrix")

# Targeted calculation of Spearman correlation and p-value
library(WGCNA)
target_data <- as.matrix(meta.gobj[, c(13:15, 18:54)])
expr.t <- t(as.matrix(expr))

# Based on estimates (all cell types + three proteins) ~ all genes, the runtime is 1.30148 hours.
start_time <- Sys.time()
result <- corAndPvalue(target_data, expr.t[, 1:100], method = "spearman")
end_time <- Sys.time() 
end_time - start_time 

# Only calculate cell type ~ All genes
result <- corAndPvalue(meta.gobj[, 18:54], expr.t, method = "spearman")
cor_values <- data.frame(result$cor) 
cor_values$cell_type <- row.names(cor_values) 
out.df.cor <- melt(cor_values, id.vars="cell_type", variable.name="gene", value.name="rho")

p_values <- data.frame(result$p)     
p_values$cell_type <- row.names(p_values) 
out.df.p <- melt(p_values, id.vars="cell_type", variable.name="gene", value.name="pvalue")

out.df <- merge(out.df.cor, out.df.p, by=c("cell_type", "gene"))
write.csv(out.df, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/cell_type__cor__genes.csv")
out.df <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/cell_type__cor__genes.csv", row.names=1)
out.df <- out.df[order(out.df$cell_type, out.df$pvalue, out.df$rho, decreasing=c(F, F, T), method = "radix"), ]
library(dplyr)
top10_per_category <- out.df %>%
  group_by(cell_type) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
write.csv(top10_per_category, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/cell_type__cor__genes_pos.top10.csv")

out.df <- out.df[order(out.df$cell_type, out.df$pvalue, out.df$rho, decreasing=c(F, F, F), method = "radix"), ]
top10_per_category <- out.df %>%
  group_by(cell_type) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
write.csv(top10_per_category, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/cell_type__cor__genes_pos.neg.top10.csv")

# Only three proteins are counted ~ All genes.
result <- corAndPvalue(meta.gobj[, 13:15], expr.t, method = "spearman")

# 提取结果
cor_values <- data.frame(result$cor)  
cor_values$protein <- row.names(cor_values) 
out.df.cor <- melt(cor_values, id.vars="protein", variable.name="gene", value.name="rho")

p_values <- data.frame(result$p)     
p_values$protein <- row.names(p_values) 
out.df.p <- melt(p_values, id.vars="protein", variable.name="gene", value.name="pvalue")

out.df <- merge(out.df.cor, out.df.p, by=c("protein", "gene"))
write.csv(out.df, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/GFAP_P2RY12_Abeta_index__cor__genes.csv")

out.df.gfap <- subset(out.df, protein=="Gfap")
out.df.gfap <- out.df.gfap[order(out.df.gfap$pvalue), ]
head(out.df.gfap, 10)
#       protein      gene        rho        pvalue
# 20441    Gfap       C4b  0.3336450  0.000000e+00
# 24036    Gfap      Gfap  0.5013772  0.000000e+00
# 21875    Gfap      Cst7  0.2307664 2.874969e-219
# 31775    Gfap     Rims3 -0.2299671 1.004242e-217
# 20544    Gfap   Camk2n2 -0.2110344 6.837090e-183
# 32485    Gfap Serpina3n  0.2028666 7.150034e-169
# 32765    Gfap   Slc17a6 -0.1958484 2.645571e-157
# 35101    Gfap    Tyrobp  0.1927454 2.496690e-152
# 29032    Gfap    Nap1l5 -0.1922208 1.697972e-151
# 34904    Gfap   Tsc22d3 -0.1887851 4.195226e-146

```


# Divide into H/L regions based on Aβ index

1. Use the threshold from the PIGs identification process.

2. Read gobj.

3. Obtain C1qa expression levels.

4. Classify spots according to Aβ threshold.

5. Compare C1qa exhaustion rates in high and low Aβ regions. Note that data slots are log-scaled.

```{r}

gobj <- readRDS("001ST/001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")

meta <- read.csv("001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta.csv", row.names=1)
all(meta$cell_ID == pDataDT(gobj)$cell_ID)
# [1] TRUE

Abeta_index_cutoff <- 10
meta$Abeta_pos <- ifelse(meta$Abeta_index > Abeta_index_cutoff, 
                          yes = "pos", no="neg")

# Obtain C1qa expression levels
expr <- get_expression_values(gobj, values="normalized", output="matrix")
meta$C1qa <- as.vector(expr["C1qa", ])

# Compare the C1qa depletion rates in regions with high and low Aβ.
AD.pos <- subset(meta, Abeta_pos=="pos" & treatment == "FADCtrl")
PL.pos <- subset(meta, Abeta_pos=="pos" & treatment == "PLX5622")

```

# Consistency of IF, ST, and SN

## GFAP.IF ~ Astro_2.DE (Subdivided brain regions).

To remove treatment effects and batch effects, sample points need to be mutually compatible. Using the currently common 11 brain regions seems too few. It's better to use the results of subdivided clustering.

```{r}
# Load IF results
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)

# Load ST expression matrix
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
expr.st <- get_expression_values(gobj, output="matrix")
meta$P2ry12.st <- as.vector(expr.st["P2ry12", ])
meta$P2ry12.st <- as.vector(expr.st["P2ry12", ])
meta$Gfap.st <- as.vector(expr.st["Gfap", ])
meta$Thy1.st <- as.vector(expr.st["Thy1", ])

# Load the deconvolution results
df.deconvo <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/cell2location/level2/spot.anno.prop.csv", row.names=1)

# Load ST sub-clustering results (only retain common clusters)
meta.subclustered <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/001preprocess/gobj.meta.subclustered.csv", row.names=1)
library(reshape2)
df <- aggregate(cell_ID~treatment+cell_types.sub, FUN=length, data=meta.subclustered)
df2 <- dcast(cell_types.sub~treatment, data=df)
df2[is.na(df2)] <- 0
df3 <- df2[apply(df2[, -1], 1, min) > 10, ] # Retain subclusters with more than 10 spots for each process.
gobj.meta.sub <- subset(meta.subclustered, cell_types.sub %in% df3$cell_types.sub)

# Merge IF results and deconvolution results
meta2 <- merge(gobj.meta.sub[, c("cell_ID", "treatment", "batch", "cell_types", "cell_types.sub")], 
      meta[, c("cell_ID", "Abeta_index", "P2ry12", "Gfap", "Thy1.st", "P2ry12.st", "Gfap.st")], 
      by = "cell_ID")

meta3 <- merge(meta2, df.deconvo[, -(2:3)], by.x="cell_ID", by.y="cell.id")

meta3 <- meta3[, -c(1,4)]

# Summarized based on specific brain regions
meta.agg <- aggregate(.~treatment+batch+cell_types.sub, FUN=mean, data=meta3)
write.csv(meta.agg, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/modal_consistency.csv", row.names=F)


# Perform correlation analysis without considering batch size or processing time.
cor.test(meta.agg$Gfap, meta.agg$Astro_0, method="spearman")
cor.test(meta.agg$Gfap, meta.agg$Astro_1, method="spearman")
cor.test(meta.agg$Gfap, meta.agg$Astro_2, method="spearman")

# After attempting to correct for batch effects and treatment effects, a correlation analysis was performed.
library(limma)
meta.agg.corrected <- removeBatchEffect(t(meta.agg[, -(1:3)]), batch=meta.agg$treatment, batch2=meta.agg$batch)
meta.agg.corrected <- data.frame(t(meta.agg.corrected))
cor.test(meta.agg.corrected$Gfap, meta.agg.corrected$Astro_0, method="spearman")
cor.test(meta.agg.corrected$Gfap, meta.agg.corrected$Astro_1, method="spearman")
cor.test(meta.agg.corrected$Gfap, meta.agg.corrected$Astro_2, method="spearman")

# Try taking only specific samples
for(treat in c("WTCtrl", "FADCtrl", "PLX5622")) {
  meta.agg.sub <- subset(meta.agg, treatment == treat)
  for (ct in c("Astro_0", "Astro_1", "Astro_2", "Micro_0_0", "Micro_0_1", "Micro_0_2")) {
    result <- cor.test(meta.agg.sub$Gfap, meta.agg.sub[[ct]], method="spearman")
    pvalue <- result$p.value
    rho <- result$estimate
    cat("Treatment:", treat, "; Cell type:", ct, "; p-value:", pvalue, "; rho:", rho, "\n")
  }
}

# Treatment: WTCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_0 ; p-value: 0.2108353 ; rho: -0.1412799
# Treatment: WTCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_1 ; p-value: 0.197709 ; rho: 0.1454055
# Treatment: WTCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_2 ; p-value: 2.37744e-06 ; rho: 0.5059541

# Treatment: FADCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_0 ; p-value: 0.02507886 ; rho: -0.2447504
# Treatment: FADCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_1 ; p-value: 6.65257e-05 ; rho: 0.4246634
# Treatment: FADCtrl ; Cell type: Astro_2 ; p-value: 3.529308e-06 ; rho: 0.4871722

# Treatment: PLX5622 ; Cell type: Astro_0 ; p-value: 0.1491301 ; rho: -0.1586919
# Treatment: PLX5622 ; Cell type: Astro_1 ; p-value: 0.4529111 ; rho: 0.08285917
# Treatment: PLX5622 ; Cell type: Astro_2 ; p-value: 0.21924 ; rho: 0.1353245

```

## Use a bar chart to display

```{r}
# 1, Use subdivided brain regions to retain only common brain regions.

# Directly use the meta.agg above.
setwd("/home/mouse_ad/001ST")
meta.agg <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/modal_consistency.csv")

#2. Describe the steps of the preceding data merging code in Chinese.

#3. Subtract the Abeta_index of the WT group from the subdivided brain regions of the AD group to obtain the corrected Aβ index for each brain region.

WT <- subset(meta.agg, treatment == "WTCtrl", select=c("batch", "cell_types.sub", "Abeta_index"))
AD <- subset(meta.agg, treatment == "FADCtrl", select=c("batch", "cell_types.sub", "Abeta_index"))
meta.agg.diff <- merge(WT, AD, by=c("cell_types.sub", "batch"), suffixes=c("_WT", "_AD"))
meta.agg.diff$Abeta_index.corrected <- meta.agg.diff$Abeta_index_AD - meta.agg.diff$Abeta_index_WT
meta.agg.diff2 <- aggregate(Abeta_index.corrected~cell_types.sub, FUN=mean, data=meta.agg.diff)
meta.agg.diff2 <- meta.agg.diff2[order(meta.agg.diff2$Abeta_index.corrected), ]

# 4. Sort the brain regions according to their corrected Aβ index to obtain High Aβ brain regions (H) and Low Aβ brain regions (L).

# Isortex and hippo have both high and low Aβ indices, making them good research materials.

# The definition criteria for H and L may need to consider the proportion of brain regions. For now, let's arbitrarily define a seemingly objective one: the top 50%.
meta.agg.diff2 <- meta.agg.diff2[order(meta.agg.diff2$Abeta_index.corrected), ]
nrow(meta.agg.diff2)
meta.agg.diff2$Abeta_group <- ifelse(1:nrow(meta.agg.diff2) <= nrow(meta.agg.diff2)/2, "L", "H")

#5. Summarize the various indicators based on H and L, and draw a bar chart.
H.region <- meta.agg.diff2$cell_types.sub[meta.agg.diff2$Abeta_group == "H"]
meta.agg$Abeta_group <- ifelse(meta.agg$cell_types.sub %in% H.region, "H", "L")

write.csv(meta.agg, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/modal_consistency.group_by_Abeta.csv")
meta.agg <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/modal_consistency.group_by_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)

# Astrocytes
library(dplyr)
# meta.agg.summary <- meta.agg %>%
#   group_by(treatment, Abeta_group) %>%
#   summarise(across(c(Gfap, Astro_0, Astro_1, Astro_2), mean))

# Microglia
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
dir.create("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/ST_SN_IF_consistency")

meta.agg.summary <- meta.agg %>%
  group_by(treatment, Abeta_group) %>%
  summarise(across(c(P2ry12, P2ry12.st, Micro_0_0, Micro_0_1, Micro_0_2), mean))

# version 1
p.list <- list()

for (i in c("P2ry12", "P2ry12.st", "Micro_0_0", "Micro_0_1", "Micro_0_2")) {
  data <- meta.agg.summary[, c("treatment", "Abeta_group", i)]
  colnames(data) <- c("treatment", "Abeta_group", "expr")
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x=Abeta_group, y=expr)) + 
    geom_bar(stat="identity") + 
    labs(x="Aβ group", y=paste0(i, " expression")) + 
    facet_grid(.~ treatment) +
    theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank(), 
          legend.position="top")
  p.list[[i]] <- p
}

pall <- wrap_plots(p.list, ncol=1)

ggsave("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/ST_SN_IF_consistency/P2RY12_Micro.png", pall, width=6, height=8)

# version 2
colnames(meta.agg.summary) <- c("treatment", "Abeta_group", "P2RY12", "P2ry12", "HOM", "DAM", "Micro_0_2")

p.list <- list()

for (i in c("P2RY12", "P2ry12", "HOM", "DAM", "Micro_0_2")) {
  data <- meta.agg.summary[, c("treatment", "Abeta_group", i)]
  data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, levels=c("WTCtrl", "FADCtrl", "PLX5622"), ordered=T)
  colnames(data) <- c("treatment", "Abeta_group", "expr")
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x=Abeta_group, y=expr, fill=treatment)) + 
    geom_bar(stat="identity") + 
    scale_fill_manual(values=c("WTCtrl"="#FBDCBF", "FADCtrl"="#F39B9A", "PLX5622"="#BBBBDE"))
  
  if (i %in% c("P2RY12", "P2ry12")){
    p <- p + labs(y=paste0(i, " Expression"))
  } else {
    p <- p + labs(y=paste0(i, " Proportion"))
  }
  
  p <- p + facet_grid(.~ treatment) +
    theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), 
          panel.background=element_blank(), 
          legend.position="none", 
          strip.text=element_blank(), 
          axis.title.x = element_blank())
  p.list[[i]] <- p
}

pall <- wrap_plots(p.list, ncol=5)

ggsave("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/ST_SN_IF_consistency/P2RY12_Micro.v2.png", pall, width=10, height=3)
# /storage/lch/project/2022-10-14_mouse_spatial/001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/ST_SN_IF_consistency

# Calculate the significance of the difference.

# Handle the differences within the range, specifically between H and L.
meta.agg.summary2 <- meta.agg %>%
  group_by(treatment, batch, Abeta_group) %>%
  summarise(across(c(P2ry12, P2ry12.st, Micro_0_0, Micro_0_1, Micro_0_2), mean))

for (Treat in c("FADCtrl", "WTCtrl", "PLX5622")){
  df <- subset(meta.agg.summary2, treatment == Treat)
  for (Feat in c("P2ry12", "P2ry12.st", "Micro_0_0", "Micro_0_1", "Micro_0_2")) {
    Y.corrected <- removeBatchEffect(t(df[[Feat]]), batch=df$batch)
    H <- Y.corrected[df$Abeta_group == "H"]
    L <- Y.corrected[df$Abeta_group == "L"]
    O <- t.test(H, L) # p=0.085
    cat("Treatment:", Treat, "; Feature:", Feat, "; p-value:", O$p.value, "\n")
  }
}

# Results Description:

# 1. Although differences between H and L are not always present, they are detectable under certain conditions.

# 2. Differences between different treatments are relatively significant.

# 3. Differences between different cell subtypes are also visible.

# 4. Some discrepancies with expectations. Surprisingly, there was no difference in P2ry12 between H and L during empty rotation, unlike IF and DE. However, this can be explained. IF represents past transcriptional expression products, ST represents

# the current transcriptional expression state (single gene), and DE represents cell proportion (inferred from multiple genes).

# Should we consider taking mononuclear cells and cell proportions into account?

# Differences between treatments?
```

## GFAP.IF ~ Astro_2.DE(all spots)。

```{r}
meta <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)

df.deconvo <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/cell2location/level2/spot.anno.prop.csv", row.names=1)
df.deconvo2 <- data.frame(df.deconvo[, 4:ncol(df.deconvo)], row.names=df.deconvo$cell.id)
df.deconvo2 <- df.deconvo2[meta$cell_ID, ]
meta.if.de <- cbind(meta, df.deconvo2)

cor.test(meta.if.de$Gfap, meta.if.de$Astro_2, method="spearman")
# data:  meta.if.de$Gfap and meta.if.de$Astro_2
# S = 8.2324e+11, p-value < 2.2e-16
# alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
# sample estimates:
#       rho
# 0.1885793

cor.test(meta.if.de$P2ry12, meta.if.de$Micro_0_0, method="spearman")
# data:  meta.if.de$P2ry12 and meta.if.de$Micro_0_0
# S = 6.7053e+11, p-value < 2.2e-16
# alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
# sample estimates:
#       rho
# 0.3390935

```


## Trem2 expression was higher in the Aβ-H brain region than in the Aβ-L brain region.

```{r}

#1, Load Aβ grouping results
meta.agg <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/modal_consistency.group_by_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)

#2, Load the ST sub-clustering results (only retain common clusters)
meta.subclustered <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/001preprocess/gobj.meta.subclustered.csv", row.names=1)

# Merge 1 and 2, based on subdivided brain region IDs
br2group <- meta.agg$Abeta_group
names(br2group) <- meta.agg$cell_types.sub
meta.subclustered.sub <- subset(meta.subclustered, cell_types.sub %in% meta.agg$cell_types.sub)
meta.subclustered.sub$Abeta_group <- br2group[meta.subclustered.sub$cell_types.sub]

# Load the ST expression matrix to extract Trem2 expression levels.
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
meta.st <- pDataDT(gobj)
expr.st <- get_expression_values(gobj, output="matrix", values="normalized")
meta.st$Trem2 <- as.vector(expr.st["Trem2", ])
meta.st$Tmem119 <- as.vector(expr.st["Tmem119", ])

#3, merge 1, 2, and 3
meta.st.sub <- merge(meta.subclustered.sub[, c("cell_ID", "Abeta_group")], 
                        meta.st[, c("cell_ID", "treatment", "batch", "Trem2", "Tmem119")], 
                        by = "cell_ID")

write.csv(meta.st.sub, "001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/compare_Trem2_in_Abeta-H_and_Abeta-L.csv", row.names=F)

meta.st.sub <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/compare_Trem2_in_Abeta-H_and_Abeta-L.csv")

# draw
meta.st.sub2 <- subset(meta.st.sub, treatment=="FADCtrl")

# v1: default boxplot
p <- ggplot(meta.st.sub2, aes(x=Abeta_group, y=Trem2, fill=treatment)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  labs(x="Aβ group", y="Trem2 Expression") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("FADCtrl"="#F39B9A")) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank(), legend.position="top")

ggsave("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/Trem2_in_Abeta-H_and_Abeta-L.png", p, width=4, height=4)
# /storage/lch/project/2022-10-14_mouse_spatial/001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/

# 统计检验。
Trem2.H <- meta.st.sub2$Trem2[meta.st.sub2$Abeta_group == "H"]
Trem2.L <- meta.st.sub2$Trem2[meta.st.sub2$Abeta_group == "L"]
t.test(Trem2.H, Trem2.L)

# Use the ggpubr package to draw box plots
library(ggpubr)
p1 <- ggboxplot(meta.st.sub2, x="Abeta_group", y="Trem2", fill="treatment", 
               palette=c("FADCtrl"="#F39B9A"), 
               xlab="Aβ group", ylab="Trem2 Expression") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("H", "L")), label = "p.signif", method = "t.test") +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank(), legend.position="none")

p2 <- ggboxplot(meta.st.sub2, x="Abeta_group", y="Tmem119", fill="treatment", 
               palette=c("FADCtrl"="#F39B9A"), 
               xlab="Aβ group", ylab="Tmem119 Expression") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("H", "L")), label = "p.signif", method = "t.test") +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Arial"), panel.background=element_blank(), legend.position="none")

pall <- p1 + p2 + plot_layout(ncol=2)
ggsave("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/Trem2_in_Abeta-H_and_Abeta-L.v2.png", pall, width=4, height=4)
```
